heading: Concealment, removal, or mutilation generally
label: 2071 Concealment, removal, or mutilation generally
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 234, 235 (, §§ 128, 129, , 1112).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 32135 Stat. 1111Section consolidates sections 234 and 235 of title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed.Reference in subsection (a) to intent to steal was omitted as covered by .section 641 of this titleMinor changes were made in phraseology.

(b). inserted at end “As used in this subsection, the term ‘office’ does not include the office held by any person as a retired officer of the Armed Forces of the United States.”Pub. L. 101–510

children.label: a
children.text: Whoever willfully and unlawfully conceals, removes, mutilates, obliterates, or destroys, or attempts to do so, or, with intent to do so takes and carries away any record, proceeding, map, book, paper, document, or other thing, filed or deposited with any clerk or officer of any court of the United States, or in any public office, or with any judicial or public officer of the United States, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than three years, or both.

children.label: b
children.text: Whoever, having the custody of any such record, proceeding, map, book, document, paper, or other thing, willfully and unlawfully conceals, removes, mutilates, obliterates, falsifies, or destroys the same, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than three years, or both; and shall forfeit his office and be disqualified from holding any office under the United States. As used in this subsection, the term “office” does not include the office held by any person as a retired officer of the Armed Forces of the United States.
